title: Zoro Faces the World’s Strongest Swordsman
description: On pages 1–4, Zoro confronts Dracule Mihawk, forcing his dream of becoming the world’s greatest swordsman into a brutal and public test.
scene: The Baratie battle pauses around a duel that dwarfs the immediate conflict: Zoro faces the gap between ambition and the world’s strongest swordsman.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I never thought I'd be able to meet you so early on...

dialogue.speaker: Mihawk
dialogue.text: A waste, if you ask me.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.text: There's not a chance someone can match our bro!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: that man... just what did he do to this ship...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Just what're you planning to do with that?

dialogue.speaker: Mihawk
dialogue.text: I'm different from those stupid brutes who'd go all out just to hunt a mere rabbit.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Though you may be a swordsman of some renown,

dialogue.speaker: Mihawk
dialogue.text: This is east blue, the weakest of the four seas,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: that are separated by red line and grand line.

dialogue.speaker: Mihawk
dialogue.text: Unfortunately, I do not have a blade smaller than this at the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: To just how much you can underestimate me...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You better not regret it when you die!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: hear me, little frog in his well.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's time for you to realize that the world is bigger than what it seems from your well.

summary: This part is a defining moment for Zoro, showing that his dream is real enough to risk death and that his loyalty to Luffy survives defeat.
